title: Per-block-kind Nunjucks macros missing for 6 newer block kinds — decisions / spec-reviews / features / framework-gaps / layer-plans / research
description:
**Per canonical-resolution analysis 2026-05-29 (closure via supersession by TASK-093):** The gap is: **six whole-block delegators missing from `shared/macros.md`** for the newer block kinds, preventing agent templates from rendering contextBlocks-injected whole-block dumps via the canonical `{% from "shared/macros.md" import render_<kind> %}` pattern. Per-item macros exist; the thin wrapper that maps over the block array and delegates to them does not. Each is ~4 lines following the established `render_whole_block_truthy`/`render_whole_block_nonempty` scaffold. Names should follow the file's plural convention: `render_decisions`, `render_features`, `render_framework_gaps`, `render_layer_plans`, `render_research`, `render_spec_reviews` (note: user's original rewrite said `render_all_research`; the implemented name is `render_research` matching the plural convention more directly — implicitly accepted via plan approval).

Original-body corrections (5 disclarities the original FGAP body propagated):
(1) Claim "no per-item macros exist for the 6 kinds" was false — per-item macros existed at `packages/pi-workflows/templates/items/<kind>.md` (14 files); the gap was specifically the absent whole-block *delegator wrappers* in shared/macros.md.
(2) Evidence set was blind to `packages/pi-workflows/templates/items/` — every items/ file existed but went uncited; evidence enumerated only schemas + the symptom + a stale CLAUDE.md note.
(3) Proposed macro names (`render_decision`, `render_feature`, etc. — singular forms) would have *collided* with the existing per-item macros at the same path; the correct convention is plural (`render_decisions`, etc.) per the file's established pattern (`render_tasks`, `render_issues`).
(4) Stale CLAUDE.md note "Per-item macros for newer block kinds ... are pending" was cited as authoritative; CLAUDE.md had drifted from the substrate state.
(5) Impact statement conflated two distinct rendering paths: per-item rendering via `render-item-by-id` (which was unaffected — items/ macros existed) vs whole-block contextBlocks-injected rendering (the actual blocked path).

evidence.reference: single-source of per-block-kind rendering macros; absent macros for the 6 listed kinds

Notes: 'Per-item macros for newer block kinds (decisions, spec-reviews, features, framework-gaps, layer-plans, research) are pending — REVIEW-001 is gated on render_decision.' — implicit gap declaration, not actionable FGAP
REVIEW-001 status not-started; cannot dispatch as JIT agent without render_decision macro
contextBlocks injection implemented; injection works for any block; gap is at the macro layer not the injection layer
block schema defines item shape that the missing render_decision macro would render
schema defines shape for missing render_framework_gaps macro
schema defines shape for missing render_feature macro
schema defines shape for missing render_layer_plan macro
schema defines shape for missing render_research macro
schema defines shape for missing render_spec_review macro

Exhaustive verbatim enumeration of 463 macros-related statements across .project substrate (decisions, framework-gaps, features, tasks, issues, research, verification) + analysis md files + CLAUDE.md; pure enumeration, no synthesis. Reference for canonical scope-grounding when acting on FGAP-037.

Persisted canonical resolution mapping FGAP-037 into the FEAT-001 consumer-cascade arc. Six-element resolution: relocate agent-template tree pi-workflows→pi-jit-agents per DEC-0049 uniform-agent axiom; add 6 missing whole-block delegators with plural-convention names at new canonical location; extend CANONICAL_MACRO_NAMES with array_key column; consumer-cascade the template-search-path; re-file FGAP-037 with corrected body; absorb closure into FEAT-001 as TASK-093.

impact: Agent templates using `{% from "shared/macros.md" import render_<kind-plural> %}` to render contextBlocks-injected whole-block dumps had no import target for 6 of the newer block kinds (decisions, features, framework-gaps, layer-plans, research, spec-reviews). Per-item rendering via `render-item-by-id` was unaffected; the gap was specifically the absent whole-block delegator wrappers. Closed by relocating the entire agent-template tree to pi-jit-agents (per DEC-0049 uniform-agent axiom) and adding the 6 missing delegators atomically at the canonical location.
canonical_vocabulary: Nunjucks template macro library — per-record-type rendering primitives
proposed_resolution: Six whole-block delegators added at `packages/pi-jit-agents/templates/shared/macros.md` (post-relocation canonical location): `render_decisions`, `render_features`, `render_framework_gaps`, `render_layer_plans`, `render_research`, `render_spec_reviews`. Each is a thin wrapper following the established `render_whole_block_truthy` scaffold, importing the per-item macro from `items/<kind>.md` and delegating with `data.<array_key>` honoring the conception.json array-key divergence (framework-gaps→gaps, layer-plans→plans, spec-reviews→reviews). CANONICAL_MACRO_NAMES registry extended to carry the array_key column. Superseded by TASK-093 (filed under FEAT-001) which lands the relocation + delegators + registry extension + consumer rewire atomically. Closing-citation: branch `feat-001-template-relocation`; runtime demo `scripts/orchestrator/runtime-demo-whole-block-delegators.ts`; adversarial probe `/tmp/fgap-037-adversarial-probe.md` (4/4 PASS); persisted canonical-resolution analysis at `analysis/2026-05-29-fgap-037-canonical-resolution.md`.
